1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Capture the active Claude session's JSONL transcript(s) into the repo for provenance.
3	
4	Run by the pre-commit hook (.githooks/pre-commit) so every commit pulls in an up-to-date
5	snapshot of the agent interactions/effort behind it — an auditable record alongside the work.
6	
7	What it captures: the *active* session (the most-recently-modified top-level transcript in the
8	Claude project dir) plus that session's ``<id>/`` subtree (subagents + workflow transcripts).
9	Other/unrelated sessions are left out. Credential-shaped secrets are redacted on the way in.
10	
11	Honest scope: transcripts still contain local filesystem paths and may contain the operator's
12	email and internal reasoning. If this repo is published, review/exclude ``transcripts/`` first
13	(see transcripts/README.md). The wheel never ships them (only ``src/assay_engine`` is packaged).
14	
15	Config: ASSAY_TRANSCRIPT_SRC overrides the source dir. Exits 0 on any error (never blocks a
16	commit).
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import datetime as _dt
22	import hashlib
23	import json
24	import os
25	import re
26	import sys
27	from pathlib import Path
28	
29	DEFAULT_SRC = Path.home() / ".claude" / "projects" / "-Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc"
30	DEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "sessions"
31	MANIFEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "MANIFEST.json"
32	
33	# Credential-shaped secrets to redact (defense). Pass 3 (#F-024) extends this to operator PII —
34	# email addresses and the home-directory username embedded in filesystem paths — so committing
35	# transcripts as provenance (the campaign mandate) does not also publish the operator's identity.
36	# Reasoning content and the path STRUCTURE remain (legitimate provenance); see the README caveat
37	# for the full public-release consideration.
38	_SECRET_PATTERNS = [
39	    re.compile(r"sk-ant-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{2,}"),  # any sk-ant- token incl. oat01 OAuth + placeholders
40	    re.compile(r"\bgh[posru]_[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}\b"),  # GitHub tokens
41	    re.compile(r"\bAKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}\b"),  # AWS access key id
42	    # bearer tokens incl. base64url/base64 JWTs — must cover + / = or the token is split at the
43	    # first such char and only the leading fragment is redacted (pass 3, #F-051).
44	    re.compile(r"(?i)bearer\s+[A-Za-z0-9._\-+/=]{20,}"),
45	    # explicit OAuth/token env values: "...OAUTH_TOKEN":"<value>" / ...KEY=<value>
46	    re.compile(r'((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)"\s*:\s*")[^"]{8,}(")'),
47	    re.compile(r"((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)=)[^\s\"']{8,}"),
48	    # operator PII (#F-024): email addresses, and the username segment of a home path
49	    # (/Users/[REDACTED] /home/[REDACTED] — the path STRUCTURE is kept, only the identity is masked.
50	    re.compile(r"\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,}\b"),  # email → [REDACTED]
51	    re.compile(r"(/(?:Users|home)/)[^/\s\"']+"),  # /Users/[REDACTED] → /Users/[REDACTED] _scrub(text: str) -> str:
56	    for pat in _SECRET_PATTERNS:
57	        if pat.groups == 2:
58	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]\2", text)
59	        elif pat.groups == 1:
60	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]", text)
61	        else:
62	            text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
63	    return text
64	
65	
66	def _active_session(src: Path) -> Path | None:
67	    """The most-recently-modified top-level transcript = the live session."""
68	    candidates = sorted(src.glob("*.jsonl"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)
69	    return candidates[0] if candidates else None
70	
71	
72	def _copy_scrubbed(srcf: Path, dstf: Path) -> dict | None:
73	    try:
74	        dstf.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
75	        raw = srcf.read_bytes()
76	        if srcf.suffix == ".jsonl":
77	            data = _scrub(raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")).encode("utf-8")
78	        else:
79	            data = raw
80	        if dstf.exists() and dstf.read_bytes() == data:
81	            pass  # unchanged — still report it in the manifest
82	        else:
83	            dstf.write_bytes(data)
84	        return {
85	            "path": str(dstf.relative_to(DEST.parent.parent)),
86	            "bytes": len(data),
87	            "sha256": hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest(),
88	        }
89	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — provenance capture must never break a commit
90	        return None
91	
92	
93	def main() -> int:
94	    src = Path(os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRANSCRIPT_SRC", str(DEFAULT_SRC)))
95	    if not src.is_dir():
96	        return 0
97	    session = _active_session(src)
98	    if session is None:
99	        return 0
100	    sid = session.stem
101	    captured: list[dict] = []
102	
103	    rec = _copy_scrubbed(session, DEST / f"{sid}.jsonl")
104	    if rec:
105	        captured.append(rec)
106	
107	    # the session's subtree: subagents/ + workflows/ transcripts
108	    subtree = src / sid
109	    if subtree.is_dir():
110	        for f in sorted(subtree.rglob("*")):
111	            if f.is_file():
112	                rel = f.relative_to(subtree)
113	                rec = _copy_scrubbed(f, DEST / sid / rel)
114	                if rec:
115	                    captured.append(rec)
116	
117	    try:
118	        MANIFEST.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
119	        MANIFEST.write_text(
120	            json.dumps(
121	                {
122	                    "captured_at": _dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc).isoformat(),
123	                    "active_session": sid,
124	                    "n_files": len(captured),
125	                    "total_bytes": sum(c["bytes"] for c in captured),
126	                    "files": captured,
127	                },
128	                indent=2,
129	            )
130	            + "\n",
131	            encoding="utf-8",
132	        )
133	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
134	        return 0
135	    print(f"captured {len(captured)} transcript file(s) for session {sid}")
136	    return 0
137	
138	
139	if __name__ == "__main__":
140	    sys.exit(main())

# ADR-0003 — Local-first, data-sovereign; self-hosted observability

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-15)

## Context

The analyzed data may be sensitive, licensed, or otherwise constrained. Reproducibility
also requires that traces and experiment records be retained. SaaS observability (managed
tracing) ships analyzed content off-box and, at corpus scale with long retention, is also
expensive.

## Decision

All engine computation, storage, tracing, experiment tracking, and the **bulk** reasoning tier
run **on-box**. Observability is **self-hosted** (OpenTelemetry → a local collector) plus a
local experiment-tracking store. The bulk reasoning tier uses a local model; no data,
embeddings, traces, or results from these paths leave the machine.

Two distinct concerns must not be conflated:

- **Billing model:** the optional **high-stakes** reasoning tier uses a fixed-cost subscription
  path (the `claude` CLI / Agent SDK with an OAuth token) — *no metered API*. `scrubbed_env()`
  strips metered credentials, off-box redirects (`ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`), and proxies.
- **Data residency:** "no metered API" is **not** the same as "on-box". The high-stakes tier
  sends prompt content (derived from the data) to a frontier model hosted by Anthropic over the
  network — it is **off-box** by construction (there is no local frontier model). A study whose
  data must never leave the box must use only the bulk/local tier or `UnconfiguredReasoningSeam`.

## Consequences

- SaaS tracing/observability is disqualified by the data-sovereignty constraint,
  independent of cost.
- The engine, storage, observability, and bulk reasoning are runnable fully offline; only the
  optional high-stakes tier depends on a remote service (and is off-box) to advance.
- The operator owns retention and the full reproducibility record locally.

1	"""Pre-registration integrity (METHODOLOGY.md §3, GOVERNANCE.md §2).
2	
3	GOVERNANCE §2 defines pre-registration as three steps: a hypothesis is (1) made specific and
4	typed, (2) **content-hashed and committed**, and (3) **timestamped against a trusted authority**
5	— *before* any confirmatory test. Steps 2–3 were a presence sentinel until now: ``Hypothesis.locked``
6	asserted only that ``locked_at`` and ``timestamp_proof`` were non-``None``, so a hand-written
7	``timestamp_proof="rfc3161:demo"`` "locked" a hypothesis and nothing stopped a study from
8	locking *after* it had seen the baseline, or from swapping the hypothesis content after locking
9	(audit pass 1, issue #6). Both firewalls (:mod:`adjudication`, :mod:`discovery`) and all of
10	:mod:`confirm` rest on the lock, so a sentinel lock made the three verdicts decorative.
11	
12	This module makes the lock mean what the method says, by construction:
13	
14	- **Content binding** — :func:`canonical_hypothesis_digest` is a type-faithful SHA-256 over a
15	  hypothesis's *decision-bearing* fields (statement, kind, origin, test, decision rule,
16	  predicted direction, source claim, params). A valid proof must attest to *this* digest, so
17	  the content the verdict is about is provably the content that was locked: change the claim,
18	  the test, or the decision rule after locking and verification fails.
19	- **Verifiable timestamp** — a pluggable :class:`TimestampAuthority` returns a
20	  :class:`VerifiedTimestamp` only if the proof genuinely binds the digest. The engine ships one
21	  real, data-sovereign authority (:class:`LocalHmacAuthority`, on-box HMAC) and the *Protocol*
22	  so a study can plug an RFC-3161 TSA. It ships **no** silent-accept authority.
23	- **Lock before confirm** — :func:`require_preregistered` rejects a lock whose attested time is
24	  not strictly before the confirmation moment, so a hypothesis cannot be "pre"-registered after
25	  the result is known.
26	
27	Honest scope (the lesson carried from ADR-0008): :class:`LocalHmacAuthority` proves content
28	integrity, time binding, and unforgeability *relative to a secret that lives on this box*.
29	Whoever holds that secret could backdate a proof — it is local tamper-evidence, **not**
30	third-party non-repudiation. A study that needs non-repudiation wires an RFC-3161 authority
31	(which sends only the digest, never the data, so it stays compatible with data sovereignty —
32	ADR-0003 — at the cost of an external dependency the study opts into). And content binding
33	covers the *structured fields*: free text inside ``statement``/``params`` is hashed (so it
34	cannot change after locking) but the engine cannot judge whether that text is *meaningful* —
35	that remains a review obligation (GOVERNANCE §2 "timestamped adaptive design").
36	"""

76	    non-raising return as a genuine attestation.

88	def _require_aware_utc(instant: _dt.datetime, what: str) -> _dt.datetime:
89	    if instant.tzinfo is None or instant.utcoffset() is None:
90	        raise PreRegistrationError(

92	        )

96	def canonical_hypothesis_digest(hypothesis: Hypothesis) -> str:
97	    """A type-faithful SHA-256 over a hypothesis's decision-bearing content **and its id**.
98	
99	    Includes ``hypothesis_id``: although it is a label, it is fixed at lock time and is the key
100	    the runners attribute verdicts by (``verdict.hypothesis_id``), so leaving it unbound would
101	    let a post-lock id swap file a verdict under an id the proof never attested (adversarial
102	    review, #80). Excludes only the lock fields themselves (``locked_at``/``timestamp_proof`` —
103	    they attest *to* this digest, so binding them would be circular).
104	
105	    Raises :class:`PreRegistrationError` if the content cannot be canonicalized reproducibly
106	    (a non-finite float or an unhashable leaf in ``params``) — the pre-registration entrypoints
107	    must surface a typed firewall error, not a bare ``ValueError``/``TypeError`` (#82).
108	    """
109	    payload = {
110	        "hypothesis_id": hypothesis.hypothesis_id,
111	        "statement": hypothesis.statement,
112	        "kind": hypothesis.kind.value,
113	        "origin": hypothesis.origin.value,
114	        "test_name": hypothesis.test_name,
115	        "decision_rule": hypothesis.decision_rule,
116	        "source_claim_id": hypothesis.source_claim_id,
117	        "predicted_direction": hypothesis.predicted_direction,
118	        "params": dict(hypothesis.params),
119	    }
120	    try:
121	        return hash_text(canonical_json(payload))
122	    except (ValueError, TypeError) as exc:
123	        raise PreRegistrationError(
124	            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} cannot be canonicalized for a content "
125	            f"digest ({exc}); pre-registration requires reproducibly hashable content "
126	            "(no non-finite floats or unhashable leaves in params)"
127	        ) from None
128	
129	
130	class LocalHmacAuthority:
131	    """A real, on-box timestamp authority binding a digest and an instant under a secret key.
132	
133	    ``stamp`` issues ``local-hmac:v1:<instant-iso>|<hmac-hex>`` where the MAC is
134	    ``HMAC-SHA256(secret, digest || instant)``. ``verify`` recomputes the MAC over the digest
135	    the engine derived from the *current* hypothesis and the instant carried in the proof, and
136	    constant-time compares. The proof therefore verifies iff the content is unchanged, the
137	    instant is unchanged, and the same secret is used — without the secret it cannot be forged
138	    for arbitrary content or time.
139	
140	    Honest scope: this is local tamper-evidence, not third-party non-repudiation — the holder of
141	    ``secret`` could backdate (see module docstring). Use an RFC-3161 authority when you need a
142	    third party to vouch for the time.
143	    """
144	
145	    authority_id = "local-hmac"
146	
147	    def __init__(self, secret: bytes) -> None:
148	        if not isinstance(secret, (bytes, bytearray)) or len(secret) < 16:
149	            raise ValueError("LocalHmacAuthority secret must be >= 16 bytes of key material")
150	        self._secret = bytes(secret)
151	
152	    def _mac(self, digest: str, instant_iso: str) -> str:
153	        msg = canonical_json(["local-hmac:v1", digest, instant_iso]).encode("utf-8")
154	        return hmac.new(self._secret, msg, "sha256").hexdigest()
155	
156	    def stamp(self, digest: str, *, instant: _dt.datetime | None = None) -> str:
157	        when = instant if instant is not None else _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
158	        when = _require_aware_utc(when, "stamp instant")
159	        instant_iso = when.isoformat()
160	        return f"{_PROOF_PREFIX}{instant_iso}{_PROOF_FIELD_SEP}{self._mac(digest, instant_iso)}"
161	
162	    def verify(self, digest: str, proof: str) -> VerifiedTimestamp:
163	        if not proof.startswith(_PROOF_PREFIX):
164	            raise PreRegistrationError(
165	                f"proof is not a {self.authority_id} v1 token (wrong prefix)"
166	            )
167	        body = proof[len(_PROOF_PREFIX) :]
168	        instant_iso, sep, mac_hex = body.partition(_PROOF_FIELD_SEP)
169	        if not sep or not instant_iso or not mac_hex:
170	            raise PreRegistrationError("malformed local-hmac proof (expected <instant>|<mac>)")
171	        # The MAC field must be exactly the hex of a SHA-256 digest. Validating its shape here
172	        # (a) rejects garbage early and (b) keeps non-ASCII/non-hex out of hmac.compare_digest,
173	        # which raises TypeError on non-ASCII str rather than returning False (#81).
174	        if len(mac_hex) != 64 or any(c not in "0123456789abcdef" for c in mac_hex):
175	            raise PreRegistrationError("malformed local-hmac proof (mac is not 64 lowercase hex)")
176	        try:
177	            parsed = _dt.datetime.fromisoformat(instant_iso)
178	        except ValueError:
179	            raise PreRegistrationError(f"proof instant is not ISO-8601: {instant_iso!r}") from None
180	        instant = _require_aware_utc(parsed, "proof instant")
181	        expected = self._mac(digest, instant_iso)
182	        if not hmac.compare_digest(expected, mac_hex):
183	            # Either the content (digest) changed since locking, the instant was tampered, or the
184	            # proof was issued under a different secret. All are pre-registration failures.
185	            raise PreRegistrationError(
186	                "proof does not bind this hypothesis's content at the stated time "
187	                "(content changed after locking, or proof was forged/issued by another authority)"
188	            )
189	        return VerifiedTimestamp(instant=instant, authority=self.authority_id, digest=digest)
190	
191	
192	def lock_hypothesis(
193	    hypothesis: Hypothesis,
194	    *,
195	    authority: StampingAuthority,
196	    instant: _dt.datetime | None = None,
197	) -> Hypothesis:
198	    """Return a locked copy of ``hypothesis`` whose proof binds its current content.
199	
200	    The engine's blessed way to pre-register: it computes the content digest, has ``authority``
201	    stamp it, and records the attested instant in ``locked_at`` so the displayed lock time and
202	    the cryptographic proof always agree. Re-locking an already-locked hypothesis is refused.
203	
204	    Scope of the seal (same honest boundary as ADR-0008): this refuses re-locking *this object*.
205	    It cannot stop a study that willfully reconstructs a hypothesis — e.g. ``dataclasses.replace``
206	    to clear the lock fields, then re-lock different content — any more than the runners can stop
207	    a builder that reflects into their frame; both need process isolation, which is out of scope.
208	    What the engine *does* guarantee is that whatever is ultimately confirmed has a proof binding
209	    its content at an attested time before confirmation. So a re-locked copy is simply a new
210	    pre-registration with a new, later timestamp — visible as such, not a silent override.
211	    """
212	    if hypothesis.locked:
213	        raise PreRegistrationError(
214	            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} is already locked; re-locking is forbidden"
215	        )
216	    digest = canonical_hypothesis_digest(hypothesis)
217	    proof = authority.stamp(digest, instant=instant)
218	    verified = authority.verify(digest, proof)  # fail loud here if the authority is misbehaving
219	    return replace(hypothesis, locked_at=verified.instant.isoformat(), timestamp_proof=proof)
220	
221	
222	def verify_preregistration(
223	    hypothesis: Hypothesis, *, authority: TimestampAuthority
224	) -> VerifiedTimestamp:
225	    """Verify a hypothesis is genuinely pre-registered: present, content-bound, time-attested.
226	
227	    Raises :class:`PreRegistrationError` if the hypothesis is not locked, the proof does not
228	    bind the *current* content digest, the attested instant is not a timezone-aware time, or the
229	    self-reported ``locked_at`` disagrees with the authority's attested instant.
230	    """
231	    if not hypothesis.locked:
232	        raise PreRegistrationError(
233	            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} is not locked; confirm is forbidden "
234	            "before pre-registration (METHODOLOGY §3 / GOVERNANCE §2)"
235	        )
236	    assert hypothesis.timestamp_proof is not None  # .locked guarantees this; for the type checker
237	    digest = canonical_hypothesis_digest(hypothesis)
238	    verified = authority.verify(digest, hypothesis.timestamp_proof)
239	    if verified.digest != digest:
240	        raise PreRegistrationError(
241	            f"authority attested digest {verified.digest!r} but the hypothesis content hashes to "
242	            f"{digest!r} — the proof does not cover this hypothesis"
243	        )
244	    # The displayed lock time must match the attested one, so a study cannot show a time the
245	    # authority did not vouch for.
246	    try:
247	        claimed = _dt.datetime.fromisoformat(hypothesis.locked_at or "")
248	    except ValueError:
249	        raise PreRegistrationError(
250	            f"locked_at {hypothesis.locked_at!r} is not an ISO-8601 timestamp"
251	        ) from None
252	    if _require_aware_utc(claimed, "locked_at") != verified.instant:
253	        raise PreRegistrationError(
254	            f"locked_at {hypothesis.locked_at!r} disagrees with the authority-attested instant "
255	            f"{verified.instant.isoformat()!r}"
256	        )
257	    return verified
258	
259	
260	def require_preregistered(
261	    hypothesis: Hypothesis,
262	    *,
263	    authority: TimestampAuthority,
264	    not_after: _dt.datetime,
265	) -> VerifiedTimestamp:
266	    """Gate a confirmatory step: the hypothesis must be verifiably locked *before* ``not_after``.
267	
268	    ``not_after`` is the confirmation moment (a runner captures it at entry). A lock whose
269	    attested instant is not strictly earlier is rejected — you cannot pre-register a hypothesis
270	    after the result that would confirm it is already in hand.
271	    """
272	    not_after = _require_aware_utc(not_after, "not_after")
273	    verified = verify_preregistration(hypothesis, authority=authority)
274	    if not verified.instant < not_after:
275	        raise PreRegistrationError(
276	            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} was locked at "
277	            f"{verified.instant.isoformat()!r}, not strictly before the confirmation moment "
278	            f"{not_after.isoformat()!r} — a lock must precede confirmation (GOVERNANCE §2)"
279	        )
280	    return verified

1	"""Tiered reasoning seam — the single abstraction over LLM execution by stakes tier.
2	
3	No engine component talks to an LLM directly; everything routes through :class:`ReasoningSeam`.
4	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's hardened ``reasoning_client`` (ARCHITECTURE.md
5	§1, ADR-0003). Two execution tiers:
6	
7	- ``StakesTier.BULK`` — a local model runtime (loopback-only, e.g. Ollama) for high-volume,
8	  low-stakes work.
9	- ``StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES`` — a frontier model via a **fixed-cost subscription** path
10	  (the ``claude`` CLI / Agent SDK using an OAuth token). No metered API key is ever used:
11	  every metered Anthropic credential is scrubbed from the subprocess environment first.
12	
13	Hardened invariants preserved from the prior platform:
14	
15	- forward-proof credential scrubbing (any ``ANTHROPIC_*KEY``/``*TOKEN`` stripped; the
16	  subscription ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` preserved);
17	- hard per-call timeouts via a bounded worker pool with a saturation guard;
18	- independent retry budgets — exponential backoff for transient errors, long backpressure
19	  for rate limits — and permanent errors never retried;
20	- robust JSON extraction via a balanced-brace walker (not a greedy regex);
21	- loopback enforcement on the local backend (ADR-0003).
22	
23	Heavy backends (``langchain_ollama``, ``claude_agent_sdk``) and tracing (``opentelemetry``)
24	are imported lazily, so this module imports — and is unit-testable by mocking ``_attempt`` —
25	with none of them installed. Install the optional ``reasoning`` extra to run real calls.
26	"""

119	def is_metered_anthropic_credential(key: str) -> bool:

127	    (``anthropic_api_key``) must scrub too — the AWS_/GOOGLE_ checks already uppercase, so this
128	    closes the one credential path that did not.

166	def scrubbed_env() -> dict[str, str]:
167	    """The process env hardened for the high-stakes subprocess (ADR-0003 / no metered API).
168	
169	    Every unsafe var (:func:`is_unsafe_subprocess_var`) is **overwritten to empty**, not merely
170	    omitted. The claude-agent-sdk builds the child env as ``{**os.environ, **options.env}`` — it
171	    *merges* over the inherited environment — so a dropped key would silently fall through to its
172	    inherited value (the metered key / off-box redirect would survive). Setting it to ``""`` in
173	    the dict we hand over guarantees the merge overwrites the inherited value (#101). The
174	    subscription OAuth token is preserved so the fixed-cost path still authenticates.
175	    """
176	    env = dict(os.environ)
177	    for k in list(env):
178	        if is_unsafe_subprocess_var(k):
179	            env[k] = ""
180	    return env

183	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
184	# Bounded timeout pool with saturation guard
185	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
186	_POOL_WORKERS = 8
187	_pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_POOL_WORKERS, thread_name_prefix="reasoning-timeout")
188	# Shut the pool down at interpreter exit (#F-043). wait=False avoids blocking exit on in-flight
189	# backend calls (already bounded by _with_timeout); without this, module globals can be torn down
190	# while a worker is mid-call, leaving behavior undefined. Mirrors checkpoint.py's atexit cleanup.
191	atexit.register(_pool.shutdown, wait=False)
192	_inflight = 0
193	_inflight_lock = threading.Lock()
194	
195	
196	def _release(future: Any) -> None:
197	    # Idempotent per future (#F-032 follow-up): decrement _inflight AT MOST ONCE for a given
198	    # future. The done-callback path and the interrupt-window fallback in _submit_bounded can BOTH
199	    # reach _release for the same future when add_done_callback appended the callback and then was
200	    # interrupted before returning — without this guard that double-decrements and over-releases a
201	    # slot (the inverse of the leak being fixed). A per-future flag set under the lock makes the
202	    # "_release runs at most once" claim true rather than aspirational.
203	    global _inflight
204	    with _inflight_lock:
205	        if getattr(future, "_assay_released", False):
206	            return
207	        try:
208	            future._assay_released = True
209	        except (AttributeError, TypeError):  # pragma: no cover - Future allows attrs; defensive
210	            pass
211	        _inflight -= 1
212	
213	
214	def _submit_bounded(fn: Callable[[], Any]) -> Any:
215	    """Submit to the shared pool, refusing if every worker slot is already in flight
216	    (hung backend calls would otherwise leak all slots)."""
217	    global _inflight
218	    with _inflight_lock:
219	        if _inflight >= _POOL_WORKERS:
220	            # Saturation is a TRANSIENT condition — surface a retryable backpressure signal so
221	            # _run_with_retries waits and re-tries rather than failing the caller permanently
222	            # (#104). Concurrent callers thus queue briefly instead of erroring out.
223	            raise RateLimitError(
224	                f"reasoning timeout pool saturated ({_inflight}/{_POOL_WORKERS} in-flight) — "
225	                "backpressure; retry shortly"
226	            )
227	        _inflight += 1
228	    try:
229	        future = _pool.submit(fn)
230	    except RuntimeError as exc:  # pool shut down at interpreter exit — normalize, don't leak (#116)
231	        with _inflight_lock:
232	            _inflight -= 1
233	        raise PermanentReasoningError(f"reasoning pool is shut down: {exc}") from exc
234	    except BaseException:
235	        with _inflight_lock:
236	            _inflight -= 1
237	        raise
238	    # Register the slot-release callback under its own guard (#F-032): if a BaseException
239	    # (KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit) fires while attaching the callback, release the slot
240	    # explicitly so a permanent leak can't accumulate and brick the pool with spurious
241	    # RateLimitErrors. If the interrupt landed AFTER add_done_callback already appended the
242	    # callback (so it will still fire on completion), the explicit _release here and the later
243	    # callback both target this future — _release is per-future idempotent, so the slot is
244	    # released exactly once either way.
245	    try:
246	        future.add_done_callback(_release)
247	    except BaseException:
248	        _release(future)
249	        raise
250	    return future
251	
252	
253	def _with_timeout(fn: Callable[[], Any], timeout: float, what: str) -> Any:
254	    """Run a sync callable with a hard wall-clock timeout via the bounded pool."""
255	    future = _submit_bounded(fn)
256	    try:
257	        return future.result(timeout=timeout)
258	    except FuturesTimeout as exc:
259	        # No future.cancel(): a ThreadPoolExecutor cannot interrupt a thread already blocked
260	        # in a backend call. The worker keeps its slot until the call returns; the bounded
261	        # pool's saturation guard is the real protection against leaked/hung slots.
262	        raise ReasoningError(f"{what} timed out after {timeout:.0f}s") from exc
263	
264	
265	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
266	# JSON extraction — balanced-brace walker (robust to fences / trailing prose)
267	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
268	def _first_balanced_json(text: str) -> str | None:
269	    """Return the first balanced ``{...}`` or ``[...]`` span, respecting string literals.
270	
271	    A greedy regex grabs from the first brace to the LAST brace, mangling replies with
272	    multiple JSON values or braces in trailing prose; this walks the structure honestly.
273	    """
274	    start = None
275	    opener = closer = ""
276	    depth = 0
277	    in_string = False
278	    escape = False
279	    for i, ch in enumerate(text):
280	        if start is None:
281	            if ch in "{[":
282	                start, opener = i, ch
283	                closer = "}" if ch == "{" else "]"
284	                depth = 1
285	            continue
286	        if in_string:
287	            if escape:
288	                escape = False
289	            elif ch == "\\":
290	                escape = True
291	            elif ch == '"':
292	                in_string = False
293	            continue
294	        if ch == '"':
295	            in_string = True
296	        elif ch == opener:
297	            depth += 1
298	        elif ch == closer:
299	            depth -= 1
300	            if depth == 0:
301	                return text[start : i + 1]
302	    return None
303	
304	
305	def extract_json(text: str) -> Any:
306	    """Parse JSON from a model reply (handles code fences and surrounding prose)."""
307	    span = _first_balanced_json(text)
308	    if span is None:
309	        raise ReasoningError("no JSON object/array found in reply")
310	    try:
311	        return json.loads(span)
312	    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
313	        raise ReasoningError(f"reply contained malformed JSON: {exc}") from exc
314	
315	
316	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
317	# Tier backends (lazy imports — module loads without them)
318	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
319	def _bulk_complete(
320	    prompt: str,
321	    system: str | None,
322	    temperature: float,
323	    model: str,
324	    json_mode: bool = False,
325	    *,
326	    seed: int | None = None,
327	    json_schema: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
328	) -> str:
329	    """Tier: BULK — local model runtime (loopback-enforced).
330	
331	    ``num_predict`` is always set (bounded generation). For JSON, a caller-supplied
332	    ``json_schema`` uses Ollama's schema-constrained decoding (the reliable path); otherwise
333	    ``json_mode`` falls back to loose ``format="json"``. ``seed`` lets a retry force a different
334	    deterministic decode without sharpening temperature.
335	    """
336	    require_loopback_url(BULK_BASE_URL, what="bulk-tier model base URL")
337	    try:
338	        from langchain_ollama import ChatOllama
339	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
340	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
341	            "bulk tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (langchain-ollama) — not installed"
342	        ) from exc
343	
344	    kwargs: dict[str, Any] = dict(
345	        model=model,
346	        base_url=BULK_BASE_URL,
347	        temperature=temperature,
348	        num_predict=BULK_NUM_PREDICT,  # bound generation length (H1)
349	        client_kwargs={"timeout": BULK_TIMEOUT},
350	    )
351	    if seed is not None:
352	        kwargs["seed"] = seed
353	    if json_schema is not None:
354	        kwargs["format"] = json_schema  # Ollama schema-constrained decoding (reliable JSON)
355	    elif json_mode:
356	        kwargs["format"] = "json"  # loose native JSON decoding (syntactic only)
357	    client = ChatOllama(**kwargs)
358	    messages: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
359	    if system:
360	        messages.append(("system", system))
361	    messages.append(("human", prompt))
362	    try:
363	        reply = client.invoke(messages)
364	    except Exception as exc:
365	        # Prefer the typed HTTP status (ollama.ResponseError / httpx carry one): 404 = the model
366	        # isn't pulled → permanent. Fall back to substring matching only if no status is exposed
367	        # (robust to upstream message-wording changes — audit M4).
368	        status = getattr(exc, "status_code", None) or getattr(
369	            getattr(exc, "response", None), "status_code", None
370	        )
371	        msg = str(exc).lower()
372	        permanent = status == 404 or any(
373	            s in msg for s in ("not found", "no such model", "try pulling")
374	        )
375	        if permanent:
376	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"bulk model {model!r} not available: {exc}") from exc
377	        raise ReasoningError(f"bulk tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
378	    content = getattr(reply, "content", reply)
379	    text = content if isinstance(content, str) else str(content)
380	    text = text.strip()
381	    if not text:  # an empty reply is not a valid result — retry, don't return "" (issue #5/R5)
382	        raise ReasoningError("bulk tier returned an empty reply")
383	    return text
384	
385	
386	def _high_stakes_auth_present() -> bool:
387	    """Subscription auth available: an exported OAuth token OR a stored CLI credentials file
388	    (the normal state after ``claude login`` without exporting a token)."""
389	    if os.environ.get(OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV):
390	        return True
391	    return (Path.home() / ".claude" / ".credentials.json").is_file()
392	
393	
394	def _high_stakes_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, model: str | None) -> str:
395	    """Tier: HIGH_STAKES — frontier model via subscription CLI/Agent SDK (no metered key)."""
396	    try:
397	        import anyio
398	        from claude_agent_sdk import (
399	            AssistantMessage,
400	            ClaudeAgentOptions,
401	            ResultMessage,
402	            TextBlock,
403	            query,
404	        )
405	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
406	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
407	            "high-stakes tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (claude-agent-sdk) — not installed"
408	        ) from exc
409	    if not _high_stakes_auth_present():
410	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
411	            f"high-stakes tier requires subscription auth ({OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV} or a stored "
412	            "~/.claude/.credentials.json); none found"
413	        )
414	
415	    options = ClaudeAgentOptions(
416	        system_prompt=system,
417	        model=model,
418	        permission_mode="dontAsk",
419	        setting_sources=[],
420	        allowed_tools=[],
421	        env=scrubbed_env(),  # ADR-0003: never pass a metered Anthropic credential
422	    )
423	
424	    parts: list[str] = []
425	    result_error: dict[str, Any] | None = None
426	
427	    async def _drain() -> None:
428	        # The CLI/SDK signals rate windows and API failures by COMPLETING the stream
429	        # (a RateLimitEvent, or a terminal ResultMessage with is_error) — NOT by raising.
430	        # We must inspect the stream, not just catch exceptions (issue #R4).
431	        #
432	        # Inner cancellation (#127): wrap the stream in anyio.fail_after so a hung subprocess is
433	        # actually CANCELLED (the cancellation propagates into query() and tears down the child),
434	        # freeing the bounded-pool worker. The outer _with_timeout only raises in the caller; it
435	        # cannot cancel this thread, so without this a hung HIGH_STAKES call would leak its slot
436	        # permanently. Fires before the outer wall-clock (HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT + 30).
437	        nonlocal result_error
438	        with anyio.fail_after(HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT):
439	            await _drain_stream()
440	
441	    async def _drain_stream() -> None:
442	        nonlocal result_error
443	        async for msg in query(prompt=prompt, options=options):
444	            if type(msg).__name__ == "RateLimitEvent":
445	                rl = getattr(getattr(msg, "rate_limit_info", None), "status", None)
446	                if rl == "rejected":
447	                    result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "RateLimitEvent rejected"}
448	                continue
449	            if getattr(msg, "status", None) == "rejected":
450	                result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "rejected"}
451	                continue
452	            if isinstance(msg, AssistantMessage):
453	                for block in getattr(msg, "content", []) or []:
454	                    if isinstance(block, TextBlock):
455	                        parts.append(block.text)
456	            elif isinstance(msg, ResultMessage):
457	                # Do not let a trailing ResultMessage clobber an already-captured 429.
458	                if getattr(msg, "is_error", False) and not (
459	                    result_error and result_error.get("status") == 429
460	                ):
461	                    result_error = {
462	                        "status": getattr(msg, "api_error_status", None),
463	                        "detail": getattr(msg, "errors", None) or getattr(msg, "stop_reason", None),
464	                    }
465	
466	    try:
467	        anyio.run(_drain)
468	    except Exception as exc:
469	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
470	
471	    if result_error is not None:
472	        status = result_error.get("status")
473	        detail = result_error.get("detail") or status
474	        if status in (429, 529):
475	            raise RateLimitError(f"high-stakes tier rate window ({status}): {detail}")
476	        if status in (401, 403):
477	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier auth failed ({status}): {detail}")
478	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier reported an error (status={status}): {detail}")
479	
480	    text = "".join(parts).strip()
481	    if not text:  # empty frontier reply is not a valid result — retry (issue #R5)
482	        raise ReasoningError("high-stakes tier returned an empty reply")
483	    return text
484	
485	
486	def _attempt(request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
487	    """Route one request to its tier backend (no retry here)."""
488	    if _truthy("ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING"):
489	        raise ReasoningError("reasoning is disabled (ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING)")
490	    p = request.params
491	    system = p.get("system")
492	    temperature = float(p.get("temperature", 0.0))
493	    model = p.get("model")
494	    json_mode = bool(p.get("_json_mode", False))
495	    seed = p.get("_seed")
496	    json_schema = p.get("json_schema")  # optional caller schema → constrained JSON decoding
497	    if request.tier is StakesTier.BULK:
498	        return cast(
499	            str,
500	            _with_timeout(
501	                lambda: _bulk_complete(
502	                    request.prompt,
503	                    system,
504	                    temperature,
505	                    model or BULK_MODEL,
506	                    json_mode,
507	                    seed=int(seed) if seed is not None else None,
508	                    json_schema=json_schema,
509	                ),
510	                BULK_TIMEOUT + 10,
511	                "bulk tier",
512	            ),
513	        )
514	    if request.tier is StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES:
515	        # The high-stakes tier runs the Claude CLI subprocess (not the in-process Anthropic SDK,
516	        # so no auto-instrumentor covers it) — emit an explicit OpenInference LLM span so Phoenix
517	        # records the model/provider for this otherwise-untraced tier (#2).
518	        llm_attrs = {
519	            "llm.provider": "anthropic",
520	            "llm.system": "anthropic",
521	            "llm.model_name": str(model or HIGH_STAKES_MODEL or "subscription-default"),
522	        }
523	        with _span("reasoning.high_stakes", llm_attrs, kind="LLM"):
524	            return cast(
525	                str,
526	                _with_timeout(
527	                    lambda: _high_stakes_complete(
528	                        request.prompt, system, model or HIGH_STAKES_MODEL
529	                    ),
530	                    HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT + 30,
531	                    "high-stakes tier",
532	                ),
533	            )
534	    raise PermanentReasoningError(f"unknown tier: {request.tier!r}")
535	
536	
537	def _sleep_bounded(secs: float, deadline: float | None) -> None:
538	    """Sleep ``secs``, but never past ``deadline`` — so backoff can't blow an overall budget.
539	
540	    If the deadline has already passed, raise instead of sleeping the full interval, so a long
541	    rate-limit backoff cannot keep a caller blocked well beyond its deadline (#102).
542	    """
543	    if deadline is not None:
544	        remaining = deadline - time.monotonic()
545	        if remaining <= 0:
546	            raise ReasoningError("reasoning deadline exceeded during backoff")
547	        secs = min(secs, remaining)
548	    time.sleep(secs)
549	
550	
551	def _run_with_retries(request: ReasoningRequest, *, deadline: float | None = None) -> str:
552	    """Execute with independent transient and rate-limit retry budgets.
553	
554	    ``deadline`` (monotonic seconds) bounds both the cumulative backoff AND entry into each
555	    retry: once the deadline has passed, no further ``_attempt`` is started (#102, #F-022). It
556	    does not interrupt an attempt already in flight — that is bounded by the per-call timeout —
557	    so total wall-clock is at most ``deadline`` plus one in-flight attempt's timeout, not
558	    ``deadline`` plus the full retry budget of per-call timeouts.
559	    """
560	    transient = 0
561	    rate = 0
562	    attempted = False
563	    while True:
564	        # Gate retry ENTRY on the deadline (#F-022): the first attempt always runs, but a retry is
565	        # not started once the deadline is past — otherwise the per-attempt timeouts could stack
566	        # well beyond the overall budget even though the backoff sleeps were bounded.
567	        if attempted and deadline is not None and time.monotonic() >= deadline:
568	            raise ReasoningError("reasoning deadline exceeded before retry")
569	        attempted = True
570	        try:
571	            return _attempt(request)
572	        except PermanentReasoningError:
573	            raise
574	        except RateLimitError:
575	            if rate >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES:
576	                raise
577	            _sleep_bounded(RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF * (rate + 1), deadline)
578	            rate += 1
579	        except ReasoningError:
580	            if transient >= MAX_RETRIES:
581	                raise
582	            _sleep_bounded(BACKOFF_BASE**transient, deadline)
583	            transient += 1
584	
585	
586	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
587	# Public seam
588	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
589	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
590	class ReasoningSeam(Protocol):
591	    """Route a reasoning request to the appropriate tier with timeouts, retries, tracing."""
592	
593	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str: ...
594	
595	
596	@contextmanager
597	def _span(name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any], *, kind: str = "AGENT") -> Iterator[None]:
598	    """Emit an OpenTelemetry span if a provider is registered; a cheap no-op otherwise.
599	
600	    Stamps the OpenInference ``openinference.span.kind`` (default ``AGENT`` — a reasoning call)
601	    so Phoenix classifies the span instead of rendering it UNKNOWN. OpenTelemetry is imported
602	    lazily, so the seam traces when the (self-hosted, on-box) observability stack is wired and is
603	    a silent no-op in tests / offline (ADR-0003).
604	    """
605	    try:
606	        from opentelemetry import trace
607	    except ImportError:
608	        yield
609	        return
610	    tracer = trace.get_tracer("assay_engine.reasoning")
611	    # start_as_current_span auto-records exceptions + sets ERROR status by default (#F-005 was a
612	    # false positive — see assay_engine.observability.tracing for the verification note).
613	    with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as span:
614	        span.set_attribute("openinference.span.kind", kind)
615	        for k, v in attributes.items():
616	            span.set_attribute(k, v)
617	        yield
618	
619	
620	class TieredReasoningSeam:
621	    """Concrete tiered seam. ``run`` returns text; ``run_json`` parses a JSON reply.
622	
623	    Backends and tracing are resolved lazily, so an instance constructs with no external
624	    dependencies; calls fail loud (``PermanentReasoningError``) if a tier's backend or its
625	    subscription auth is absent. When the observability stack is present, each call emits an
626	    OpenTelemetry span; otherwise tracing is a no-op.
627	    """
628	
629	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
630	        attrs = {"assay.tier": request.tier.value, "assay.purpose": request.purpose}
631	        with _span("reasoning.run", attrs):
632	            return _run_with_retries(request)
633	
634	    def run_json(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> Any:
635	        """Generate and parse JSON, re-rolling on parse failure.
636	
637	        If the caller supplies ``params["json_schema"]``, the BULK tier uses Ollama's
638	        schema-constrained decoding (the reliable path). Otherwise it falls back to loose JSON
639	        mode + prompt instruction. BULK decoding can be deterministic (temp 0), so re-issuing the
640	        identical prompt would yield a byte-identical bad reply; each retry **varies the seed**
641	        (forcing a different deterministic decode) and *monotonically* raises temperature toward
642	        1.0 — never lowering it, so re-rolls never sharpen back toward the same output. ``params``
643	        is frozen, so each attempt builds a fresh request (issue #R6).
644	        """
645	        base_temp = float(request.params.get("temperature", 0.0))
646	        system = request.params.get("system")
647	        json_system = (
648	            f"{system}\n\nRespond with ONLY a single JSON value, no prose."
649	            if system
650	            else "Respond with ONLY a single JSON value, no prose."
651	        )
652	        last: ReasoningError | None = None
653	        deadline = time.monotonic() + RUN_JSON_DEADLINE  # overall bound across all re-rolls (#102)
654	        with _span(
655	            "reasoning.run_json",
656	            {"assay.tier": request.tier.value, "assay.purpose": request.purpose},
657	        ):
658	            for attempt in range(JSON_REROLLS + 1):
659	                if attempt > 0 and time.monotonic() >= deadline:
660	                    raise last or ReasoningError("run_json exceeded its overall deadline")
661	                temp = min(1.0, base_temp + 0.2 * attempt)  # monotonic, never decreases (M3)
662	                params = dict(request.params)
663	                params.update(temperature=temp, system=json_system, _json_mode=True, _seed=attempt)
664	                attempt_req = ReasoningRequest(
665	                    prompt=request.prompt,
666	                    tier=request.tier,
667	                    purpose=request.purpose,
668	                    params=params,
669	                )
670	                # A transient/rate/permanent error is already handled (and bounded by the shared
671	                # deadline) inside _run_with_retries — let it PROPAGATE rather than re-rolling, so
672	                # the budgets do NOT multiply (#102). We re-roll ONLY on a JSON *parse* failure.
673	                text = _run_with_retries(attempt_req, deadline=deadline)
674	                try:
675	                    return extract_json(text)
676	                except ReasoningError as exc:  # parse failure → re-roll with a new seed
677	                    last = exc
678	            raise last if last is not None else ReasoningError("run_json failed")
679	
680	    @staticmethod
681	    def is_available(tier: StakesTier) -> bool:
682	        """Cheap liveness check (spends no LLM turn). Never raises — returns False on error."""
683	        if _truthy("ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING"):
684	            return False
685	        try:
686	            if tier is StakesTier.BULK:
687	                require_loopback_url(BULK_BASE_URL, what="bulk-tier model base URL")
688	                import urllib.request
689	
690	                with urllib.request.urlopen(f"{BULK_BASE_URL}/api/tags", timeout=2) as resp:
691	                    return bool(resp.status == 200)
692	            if tier is StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES:
693	                import shutil
694	
695	                return shutil.which("claude") is not None and _high_stakes_auth_present()
696	        except Exception:
697	            return False
698	        return False
699	
700	
701	class UnconfiguredReasoningSeam:
702	    """Explicit fail-loud seam for contexts that must not perform reasoning."""
703	
704	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
705	        raise NotImplementedError(
706	            "this context uses an UnconfiguredReasoningSeam — supply a TieredReasoningSeam "
707	            "(or a test double) to perform reasoning"
708	        )

1	"""Data-sovereignty helper: enforce that network endpoints are loopback-only (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Every engine component that takes a host/URL for an on-box service (local model runtime,
4	tracing collector, vector store, checkpoint database) routes it through here so a non-local
5	address fails loud rather than silently shipping data off the machine.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	import ipaddress
11	import re
12	from urllib.parse import parse_qs, urlparse
13	
14	# A libpq keyword/value token: ``key = value`` with optional whitespace around ``=`` (libpq
15	# permits it) and an optionally single/double-quoted value (which may contain spaces).
16	_DSN_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"(\w+)\s*=\s*('[^']*'|\"[^\"]*\"|\S+)")
17	
18	
19	class NonLocalEndpointError(RuntimeError):
20	    """Raised when a configured endpoint is not loopback (would leave the machine)."""
21	
22	
23	def is_loopback_host(host: str | None) -> bool:
24	    """True only for genuine loopback hosts.
25	
26	    Parses the host as an IP and tests the loopback network (127.0.0.0/8, ::1), so a public
27	    name that merely *starts with* ``127.`` (e.g. ``127.0.0.1.evil.com``) is correctly
28	    rejected. The literal ``localhost`` is the only accepted non-IP host.
29	    """
30	    if not host:
31	        return False
32	    h = host.strip().lower()
33	    if h == "localhost":
34	        return True
35	    try:
36	        return ipaddress.ip_address(h.strip("[]")).is_loopback
37	    except ValueError:
38	        return False
39	
40	
41	def require_loopback_url(url: str, *, what: str) -> str:
42	    """Return ``url`` if its host is loopback; else raise :class:`NonLocalEndpointError`."""
43	    host = urlparse(url).hostname
44	    if not is_loopback_host(host):
45	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
46	            f"{what} must be loopback-only (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); got host {host!r} "
47	            f"from {url!r}"
48	        )
49	    return url
50	
51	
52	def require_loopback_host(host: str, *, what: str) -> str:
53	    """Return ``host`` if it is loopback; else raise. (``0.0.0.0`` binds all interfaces and
54	    is correctly rejected — it would expose data off-box.)"""
55	    if not is_loopback_host(host):
56	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
57	            f"{what} must be a loopback host (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); got {host!r}"
58	        )
59	    return host
60	
61	
62	def _authority_hosts(netloc: str) -> list[str]:
63	    """Every host in a URI authority, supporting libpq's comma-separated multi-host form.
64	
65	    ``urlparse().hostname`` returns only the *first* host of a multi-host authority
66	    (``host1:port1,host2:port2``), so a remote second host would slip past a single-host
67	    check (audit #D3). Each host may carry its own ``:port`` and IPv6 hosts are bracketed.
68	
69	    Userinfo is stripped at the **first** ``@`` — matching libpq, which (unlike ``urlparse``,
70	    which uses the last ``@``) treats the first ``@`` as the userinfo delimiter. A string like
71	    ``[REDACTED]:5432@localhost`` is therefore read as host ``evil.com`` here, not
72	    ``localhost`` (audit #D5). Any host element still containing ``@`` after that is ambiguous
73	    and is returned verbatim so the caller's loopback check fails it closed.
74	    """
75	    if "@" in netloc:
76	        netloc = netloc.split("@", 1)[1]
77	    hosts: list[str] = []
78	    for part in netloc.split(","):
79	        p = part.strip()
80	        if not p:
81	            continue
82	        if p.startswith("["):  # [ipv6] or [ipv6]:port
83	            end = p.find("]")
84	            hosts.append(p[1:end] if end != -1 else p[1:])
85	        else:
86	            hosts.append(p.rsplit(":", 1)[0] if ":" in p else p)
87	    return hosts
88	
89	
90	def require_local_uri(uri: str, *, what: str) -> str:
91	    """Return ``uri`` if it is local (ADR-0003), else raise :class:`NonLocalEndpointError`.
92	
93	    Three forms are recognized, validated against what the real client (libpq for postgres,
94	    urllib for http, sqlite for files) actually connects to — not merely what ``urlparse``
95	    reports, since the two can disagree:
96	
97	    - **URI with an authority** (``scheme://host…`` or scheme-relative ``//host…``): *every*
98	      host in the authority must be loopback (incl. each of a comma-separated multi-host
99	      authority, #D3), AND any ``host``/``hostaddr`` query parameter that libpq would use to
100	      override the authority host (#D2). ``hostaddr`` is checked independently of ``host``.
101	    - **libpq keyword/value DSN** (no authority but ``key=value`` tokens): ``host``/``hostaddr``
102	      must be loopback, tolerating libpq's whitespace/quoting around ``=`` (#P1, #D1).
103	    - **bare path** (no authority, no ``=``): a local file/dir — but a Windows UNC path
104	      (``\\\\server\\share``) is rejected as remote.
105	
106	    Inputs that cannot be parsed safely fail closed (rejected), never accepted.
107	    """
108	    try:
109	        parsed = urlparse(uri)
110	        netloc = parsed.netloc
111	        query = parsed.query
112	    except ValueError:
113	        # e.g. a malformed IPv6 authority — we cannot determine the host, so refuse.
114	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
115	            f"{what}: endpoint could not be parsed safely; rejecting (ADR-0003)"
116	        ) from None
117	
118	    if netloc:
119	        # A backslash never legitimately appears in a URI authority; a WHATWG-normalizing
120	        # client (e.g. some HTTP stacks) treats '\' as '/', so it could read a different host
121	        # than urlparse. Refuse rather than risk that differential (audit #D6, defense-in-depth).
122	        if "\\" in netloc:
123	            raise NonLocalEndpointError(
124	                f"{what}: backslash in URI authority is not permitted (ADR-0003); rejecting"
125	            )
126	        for h in _authority_hosts(netloc):
127	            # A residual '@' means the authority is ambiguous between this parser and libpq —
128	            # fail closed (audit #D5).
129	            if "@" in h or not is_loopback_host(h):
130	                raise NonLocalEndpointError(
131	                    f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); authority "
132	                    f"names non-loopback host {h!r}"
133	                )
134	        for key in ("host", "hostaddr"):
135	            for val in parse_qs(query).get(key, []):
136	                h = val.strip()
137	                if h and not is_loopback_host(h):
138	                    raise NonLocalEndpointError(
139	                        f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); URI query "
140	                        f"{key}={h!r} points off-box"
141	                    )
142	        return uri
143	
144	    if "=" in uri:  # libpq keyword/value DSN (no URI authority)
145	        for match in _DSN_TOKEN_RE.finditer(uri):
146	            if match.group(1).strip().lower() in {"host", "hostaddr"}:
147	                h = match.group(2).strip().strip("'\"")
148	                if h and not is_loopback_host(h):
149	                    raise NonLocalEndpointError(
150	                        f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); DSN names "
151	                        f"non-loopback host {h!r}"
152	                    )
153	        return uri
154	
155	    stripped = uri.lstrip()
156	    # Windows treats ANY two leading separators (in any mix of '\' and '/') as a UNC prefix
157	    # to a remote SMB share — \\srv, //srv, /\srv, \/srv all resolve off-box (audit #D6).
158	    # (The pure '//host' form is already caught by the authority branch above; covering it
159	    # here too keeps the branch self-contained.)
160	    if len(stripped) >= 2 and stripped[0] in "\\/" and stripped[1] in "\\/":
161	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
162	            f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); UNC-style path "
163	            f"{uri!r} is a remote share"
164	        )
165	    return uri  # bare path / dir store
166

1	"""Type-faithful canonical hashing — the engine's single content-digest primitive.
2	
3	Two unrelated places need a *stable, type-faithful* content hash: the baseline determinism
4	harness (so the same inputs reproduce the same baseline) and pre-registration (so a lock
5	binds the exact hypothesis content). Both must distinguish values that merely share a
6	``str()`` — ``int 1`` from ``str "1"``, ``date(2020,1,1)`` from ``"2020-01-01"`` — or the
7	hash is a liability rather than a guarantee.
8	
9	This module is the one implementation, hoisted here so it sits *below* both
10	``methodology`` and ``baseline`` in the import graph (neither may depend on the other). It is
11	dependency-free and pure. Anything it cannot canonicalize reproducibly is refused **loudly**:
12	a default (address-based) ``repr`` would make the digest differ every process and silently
13	break the guarantee it exists to provide.
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import datetime as _dt
19	import decimal
20	import hashlib
21	import json
22	import uuid
23	from typing import Any, Mapping
24	
25	
26	def keytag(key: Any) -> str:
27	    """A type-faithful, sortable string for a mapping key, so distinct keys that happen to
28	    share a ``str()`` (e.g. int ``1`` vs str ``"1"``) do NOT collide."""
29	    return f"{type(key).__name__}::{key!r}"
30	
31	
32	def canonical_plain(value: Any) -> Any:
33	    """Canonicalize ``value`` into a fully JSON-native, type-faithful structure for hashing.
34	
35	    Mappings become a sorted list of ``[typed-key, value]`` pairs; bytes, sets, and any
36	    non-JSON-native leaf are tagged with their type rather than stringified. Nothing is coerced
37	    with ``str()``, so the content hash distinguishes values that share a ``str()``.
38	    """
39	    if value is None or isinstance(value, (str, bool, int, float)):
40	        return value  # JSON-native scalars; json encodes 1, "1", true, 1.0 distinctly
41	    if isinstance(value, bytes):
42	        return {"__bytes__": value.hex()}
43	    if isinstance(value, Mapping):
44	        pairs = sorted(
45	            ([keytag(k), canonical_plain(v)] for k, v in value.items()), key=lambda p: p[0]
46	        )
47	        return {"__map__": pairs}
48	    if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
49	        return [canonical_plain(v) for v in value]
50	    if isinstance(value, (set, frozenset)):
51	        return {"__set__": sorted((canonical_plain(v) for v in value), key=repr)}
52	    # A small allowlist of leaf types with a value-faithful canonical form.
53	    if isinstance(value, (_dt.date, _dt.datetime, _dt.time)):
54	        return {"__temporal__": value.isoformat()}
55	    if isinstance(value, decimal.Decimal):
56	        return {"__decimal__": str(value)}
57	    if isinstance(value, uuid.UUID):
58	        return {"__uuid__": str(value)}
59	    # Anything else is refused LOUDLY rather than repr-tagged: a default (address-based) repr
60	    # would make the digest differ every process, silently breaking the guarantee.
61	    raise TypeError(
62	        f"cannot canonicalize a leaf of type "
63	        f"{type(value).__module__}.{type(value).__qualname__!r} reproducibly — convert it to a "
64	        "JSON-native value, bytes, date/datetime/Decimal/UUID before hashing"
65	    )
66	
67	
68	def canonical_json(value: Any) -> str:
69	    """The canonical JSON text of ``value`` — stable across processes and key orderings."""
70	    return json.dumps(canonical_plain(value), sort_keys=True, allow_nan=False)
71	
72	
73	def hash_bytes(data: bytes) -> str:
74	    return hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest()
75	
76	
77	def hash_text(text: str) -> str:
78	    return hash_bytes(text.encode("utf-8"))
79	
80	
81	def hash_value(value: Any) -> str:
82	    """Stable, type-faithful content hash of an arbitrary value.
83	
84	    ``canonical_plain`` canonicalizes everything to JSON-native form first, so ``json.dumps``
85	    needs no ``default=`` coercion — anything it still cannot encode is a real bug to surface,
86	    not to silently stringify.
87	    """
88	    return hash_text(canonical_json(value))
89

1	"""Append-only provenance trail (GOVERNANCE §3, W3C PROV-DM style).
2	
3	Every action a study takes is recorded, in order, before the next executes: ingestion, the
4	blind baseline build, each discovered/locked hypothesis, every gate decision, each verdict,
5	the source scorecard, the final report. The trail is the spine of reproducibility and of the
6	independence claim — "no step can be retroactively removed or reordered" (GOVERNANCE §3).
7	
8	The trail enforces that structurally, not by promise:
9	
10	- **Append-only.** Entries are added through :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.record` only; there is no
11	  remove/edit/reorder API and the exposed view is an immutable tuple of frozen entries.
12	- **Chained.** Each entry carries ``prev_hash`` and an ``entry_hash`` over
13	  ``(prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp)`` using the engine's one type-faithful
14	  serializer (:mod:`assay_engine._canonical`), rooted at a fixed genesis.
15	
16	Integrity — honest scope, two tiers:
17	
18	- **Unkeyed (default).** The chain is plain SHA-256. It is *tamper-evident against naive
19	  tampering and accidental corruption*: editing one entry without recomputing downstream
20	  hashes, reordering, or deleting an entry is caught by :func:`verify_records`. It is **not**
21	  forgery-proof: a motivated party who controls the serialized bytes can edit a payload and
22	  recompute the whole genesis-rooted chain, and the result verifies. Unkeyed integrity buys
23	  "is this a valid chain?", not "is this *the* chain the engine produced."
24	- **Keyed (``secret=...``).** Pass an on-box secret (as pre-registration does, ADR-0009) and
25	  the chain is HMAC-SHA256. The head cannot be recomputed without the secret, so a downstream
26	  store cannot silently rewrite history and re-seal it; :func:`verify_records` must be given
27	  the same secret. This is local tamper-*resistance*; non-repudiable third-party attestation of
28	  the trail's time remains the same pluggable concern as pre-registration, out of scope here.
29	"""
30	
31	from __future__ import annotations
32	
33	import datetime as _dt
34	import hashlib
35	import hmac
36	import threading
37	from dataclasses import dataclass
38	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping, cast
39	
40	from assay_engine._canonical import canonical_json
41	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping, unfreeze
42	
43	_UTC = _dt.timezone.utc
44	_GENESIS = "assay-provenance:v1:genesis"
45	
46	Clock = Callable[[], _dt.datetime]
47	
48	
49	class ProvenanceError(RuntimeError):
50	    """The provenance trail is inconsistent (a broken chain) or an entry cannot be recorded."""
51	
52	
53	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
54	class ProvenanceEntry:
55	    """One immutable, chained record of a single action."""
56	
57	    seq: int
58	    kind: str
59	    summary: str
60	    payload: Mapping[str, Any]
61	    timestamp: str  # ISO-8601 UTC
62	    prev_hash: str
63	    entry_hash: str
64	
65	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
66	        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", freeze_mapping(self.payload))
67	
68	
69	def _digest(
70	    seq: int,
71	    kind: str,
72	    summary: str,
73	    payload: Mapping[str, Any],
74	    timestamp: str,
75	    prev_hash: str,
76	    secret: bytes | None,
77	) -> str:
78	    try:
79	        msg = canonical_json(
80	            ["assay-prov:v1", prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, dict(payload), timestamp]
81	        ).encode("utf-8")
82	    except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
83	        # RecursionError (deeply nested payload) is a RuntimeError, not Value/TypeError — catch it
84	        # too so the entrypoints surface a typed ProvenanceError, never a raw exception (#89).
85	        raise ProvenanceError(
86	            f"provenance payload for {kind!r} cannot be serialized reproducibly ({exc}); "
87	            "record only JSON-native / hashable values of bounded depth"
88	        ) from None
89	    if secret is None:
90	        return hashlib.sha256(msg).hexdigest()
91	    return hmac.new(secret, msg, "sha256").hexdigest()
92	
93	
94	def _utc_now() -> _dt.datetime:
95	    return _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
96	
97	
98	class ProvenanceTrail:
99	    """An append-only, chained audit trail for one study run.
100	
101	    ``secret`` (>= 16 bytes) keys the chain with HMAC so a serialized trail cannot be forged
102	    without it (see module docstring). ``clock`` injects the timestamp source (deterministic
103	    tests); it must return a timezone-aware ``datetime``.
104	    """
105	
106	    def __init__(self, *, secret: bytes | None = None, clock: Clock | None = None) -> None:
107	        if secret is not None and (not isinstance(secret, (bytes, bytearray)) or len(secret) < 16):
108	            raise ValueError("provenance secret must be >= 16 bytes of key material")
109	        self._entries: list[ProvenanceEntry] = []
110	        self._secret: bytes | None = bytes(secret) if secret is not None else None
111	        self._clock: Clock = clock or _utc_now
112	        # serialize the read-compute-append so concurrent record() calls cannot interleave and
113	        # corrupt the seq/prev_hash chain (#114)
114	        self._lock = threading.Lock()
115	
116	    def record(self, kind: str, summary: str, **payload: Any) -> ProvenanceEntry:
117	        """Append one entry. Returns it. The only way to add to the trail. Thread-safe."""
118	        if not kind:
119	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance entry kind must be non-empty")
120	        with self._lock:
121	            return self._record_locked(kind, summary, payload)
122	
123	    def _record_locked(
124	        self, kind: str, summary: str, payload: Mapping[str, Any]
125	    ) -> ProvenanceEntry:
126	        seq = len(self._entries)
127	        prev_hash = self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
128	        try:
129	            instant = self._clock()
130	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the clock is an injected dependency
131	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance clock raised: {exc}") from None
132	        if not isinstance(instant, _dt.datetime):
133	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a datetime")
134	        try:
135	            # utcoffset()/astimezone()/isoformat() can raise on a hostile datetime subclass or an
136	            # out-of-range value — keep the typed-error contract (#96).
137	            if instant.tzinfo is None or instant.utcoffset() is None:
138	                raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a timezone-aware datetime")
139	            timestamp = instant.astimezone(_UTC).isoformat()
140	        except ProvenanceError:
141	            raise
142	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
143	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance timestamp could not be normalized: {exc}") from None
144	        entry_hash = _digest(seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp, prev_hash, self._secret)
145	        try:
146	            entry = ProvenanceEntry(
147	                seq=seq,
148	                kind=kind,
149	                summary=summary,
150	                payload=payload,
151	                timestamp=timestamp,
152	                prev_hash=prev_hash,
153	                entry_hash=entry_hash,
154	            )
155	        except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
156	            raise ProvenanceError(
157	                f"provenance entry for {kind!r} could not be frozen ({exc})"
158	            ) from None
159	        self._entries.append(entry)
160	        return entry
161	
162	    def as_recorder(self) -> Callable[[Any], None]:
163	        """A :data:`gates.ProvenanceRecorder` bound to this trail (records GateDecisions).
164	
165	        Reads each attribute exactly once and defensively, so a booby-trapped decision object
166	        (a property that raises) surfaces a typed ``ProvenanceError`` rather than a raw error.
167	        """
168	
169	        def _record(decision: Any) -> None:
170	            try:
171	                gate = getattr(decision, "gate", None)
172	                approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
173	                reason = getattr(decision, "reason", "")
174	                evidence = dict(getattr(decision, "evidence", {}) or {})
175	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the decision is on the trust boundary
176	                raise ProvenanceError(
177	                    f"could not read gate decision for provenance: {exc}"
178	                ) from None
179	            self.record(
180	                "gate",
181	                f"gate {gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
182	                gate=gate,
183	                approved=approved,
184	                reason=reason,
185	                evidence=evidence,
186	            )
187	
188	        return _record
189	
190	    @property
191	    def entries(self) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
192	        return tuple(self._entries)
193	
194	    @property
195	    def head(self) -> str:
196	        """The current chain head (last entry_hash, or the genesis if empty)."""
197	        return self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
198	
199	    def __len__(self) -> int:
200	        return len(self._entries)
201	
202	    def verify(self) -> None:
203	        """Recompute the chain; raise :class:`ProvenanceError` on any inconsistency."""
204	        verify_records(self._entries, secret=self._secret)
205	
206	    def to_records(self) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
207	        """Serialize to plain dicts (for a persistent store); re-checkable via :func:`verify_records`."""
208	        return entries_to_records(self._entries)
209	
210	
211	def entries_to_records(
212	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
213	) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
214	    """Serialize loose entries (e.g. ``StudyResult.provenance``) to plain dicts (#F-045).
215	
216	    The same shape :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.to_records` produces, so a caller holding only the
217	    returned entries — not the trail object — can still persist a re-checkable trail.
218	    """
219	    return tuple(
220	        {
221	            "seq": e.seq,
222	            "kind": e.kind,
223	            "summary": e.summary,
224	            # deep-thaw nested FrozenDict/tuple values so the record is plain + JSON-serializable
225	            # (#F-045): dict(e.payload) only shallow-converts and leaves nested FrozenDicts (e.g.
226	            # the baseline 'determinism' map) which json.dumps cannot encode.
227	            "payload": cast("dict[str, Any]", unfreeze(e.payload)),
228	            "timestamp": e.timestamp,
229	            "prev_hash": e.prev_hash,
230	            "entry_hash": e.entry_hash,
231	        }
232	        for e in entries
233	    )
234	
235	
236	def verify_records(
237	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
238	    *,
239	    secret: bytes | None = None,
240	) -> None:
241	    """Verify a sequence of entries forms an intact chain (order, linkage, content).
242	
243	    Pass the same ``secret`` the trail was keyed with; an unkeyed trail verifies with
244	    ``secret=None``. A keyed trail will not verify under the wrong/absent secret.
245	    """
246	    prev = _GENESIS
247	    for i, e in enumerate(entries):
248	        if e.seq != i:
249	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} has seq {e.seq} (reordered or removed)")
250	        if e.prev_hash != prev:
251	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} prev_hash breaks the chain")
252	        recomputed = _digest(e.seq, e.kind, e.summary, e.payload, e.timestamp, e.prev_hash, secret)
253	        if not hmac.compare_digest(recomputed, e.entry_hash):
254	            raise ProvenanceError(
255	                f"provenance entry {i} content was altered, or the wrong key was used (hash mismatch)"
256	            )
257	        prev = e.entry_hash
258	
259	
260	def from_records(
261	    records: "list[Mapping[str, Any]] | tuple[Mapping[str, Any], ...]",
262	    *,
263	    secret: bytes | None = None,
264	) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
265	    """Rebuild entries from :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.to_records` output and verify the chain."""
266	    entries = tuple(
267	        ProvenanceEntry(
268	            seq=r["seq"],
269	            kind=r["kind"],
270	            summary=r["summary"],
271	            payload=r["payload"],
272	            timestamp=r["timestamp"],
273	            prev_hash=r["prev_hash"],
274	            entry_hash=r["entry_hash"],
275	        )
276	        for r in records
277	    )
278	    verify_records(entries, secret=secret)
279	    return entries
280

1	"""Data versioning — content-addressed, local, deterministic (METHODOLOGY.md §7, ADR-0003).
2	
3	Every finding must cite the exact bytes it was computed from. This provides a dependency-free,
4	on-box content-addressing store: an artifact is hashed (SHA-256) and copied under a local
5	store keyed by that hash, so the same bytes always yield the same version id and any artifact
6	can be re-fetched by id. (The prior platform used DVC via an external tool; this keeps the
7	engine self-contained — an adapter may swap in DVC by implementing the same Protocol.)
8	"""
9	
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	import hashlib
13	import os
14	import re
15	import shutil
16	import tempfile
17	from pathlib import Path
18	from typing import Protocol
19	
20	_CHUNK = 1 << 20  # 1 MiB
21	_SHA256_HEX = re.compile(r"[0-9a-f]{64}")
22	
23	
24	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
25	class DataVersioner(Protocol):
26	    def put(self, path: str) -> str:
27	        """Version the artifact at ``path``; return its content hash / version id."""
28	        ...
29	
30	    def fingerprint(self, path: str) -> str:
31	        """Return the content hash of ``path`` without storing it."""
32	        ...
33	
34	
35	def _hash_file(path: Path) -> str:
36	    h = hashlib.sha256()
37	    with path.open("rb") as f:
38	        for chunk in iter(lambda: f.read(_CHUNK), b""):
39	            h.update(chunk)
40	    return h.hexdigest()
41	
42	
43	class LocalDataVersioner:
44	    """Content-addressed local artifact store. Deterministic and fully offline.
45	
46	    ``store_dir`` defaults to ``ASSAY_DATA_STORE`` or ``./.assay-data``. Files are stored under
47	    ``<store>/<aa>/<full-hash>`` (sharded by the first two hex chars).
48	    """
49	
50	    def __init__(self, store_dir: str | None = None) -> None:
51	        root = store_dir or os.environ.get("ASSAY_DATA_STORE") or str(Path.cwd() / ".assay-data")
52	        self._root = Path(root)
53	
54	    def fingerprint(self, path: str) -> str:
55	        p = Path(path)
56	        if not p.is_file():
57	            raise FileNotFoundError(f"cannot version a non-file path: {path!r}")
58	        return _hash_file(p)
59	
60	    def put(self, path: str) -> str:
61	        """Store the artifact under its content hash; return the hash (version id).
62	
63	        Idempotent: storing the same bytes twice is a no-op returning the same id. Publish is
64	        atomic — content is copied to a *per-writer-unique* temp file (so two processes
65	        storing the same digest never clobber a shared temp) and then ``os.replace``-d into
66	        place, so a reader never observes a partial artifact. The store is content-addressed,
67	        so an interrupted/duplicate write is harmless.
68	        """
69	        digest = self.fingerprint(path)
70	        dest = self._root / digest[:2] / digest
71	        if dest.exists():
72	            return digest
73	        dest.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
74	        fd, tmp_name = tempfile.mkstemp(dir=str(dest.parent), prefix=".tmp-")
75	        os.close(fd)
76	        tmp = Path(tmp_name)
77	        try:
78	            shutil.copy2(path, tmp)
79	            os.replace(tmp, dest)  # atomic publish within the same filesystem
80	        finally:
81	            tmp.unlink(missing_ok=True)  # no-op once replaced; cleans up on copy failure
82	        return digest
83	
84	    def path_for(self, digest: str) -> Path:
85	        """Local path of a previously-stored artifact by its version id.
86	
87	        The version id MUST be a SHA-256 hex digest (the only form :meth:`put` ever returns).
88	        Validating it rejects path-traversal payloads (e.g. ``../../etc/passwd``) that would
89	        otherwise compose a path outside the store (#112).
90	        """
91	        if not _SHA256_HEX.fullmatch(digest):
92	            raise ValueError(
93	                f"invalid version id {digest!r} (expected a 64-char sha256 hex digest)"
94	            )
95	        return self._root / digest[:2] / digest
96
